evidence: Our research and development efforts are strategically centered on expanding our leadership within the enterprise security industry through AI-powered innovation.
Our engineering teams apply deep expertise in AI and machine learning across networking security, cloud security, endpoint security, and security operations.
in July 2025, we completed the acquisition of Protect AI, Inc...which we expect will enhance the capabilities of our AI security platform.
Cortex XSIAM automates data integration, analysis, and triage to respond to most alerts, enabling analysts to focus on only the incidents that require human intervention.
Prisma AIRS is a comprehensive AI security platform...providing AI model scanning, posture management, red teaming, run-time security, and AI agent security.
SCM leverages AI to simplify and strengthen network security by enabling customers to proactively pinpoint vulnerabilities, gain real-time remediation recommendations.
AI algorithms may have flaws, and datasets used to train models may be insufficient or contain biased information.
These efforts could subject us to regulatory risk, legal liability, including under legislation regulating AI in jurisdictions such as the E.U.

The intellectual property ownership and license rights surrounding AI technologies...may result in exposure to claims by third parties of copyright infringement or other intellectual property misappropriation.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No dollar amounts, headcount figures, or quantified R&D/capex tied specifically to AI are disclosed in the excerpts; score capped at 3 despite named programs and an AI-focused acquisition.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Multiple named AI products described as deployed and in production with operational detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., % improvement, time saved, volume handled) appear in the excerpts; score capped at 4.
capex_mentions: No specific dollar figures for AI-attributable investment found in the excerpts.
